final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing technical infographic illustrating step-by-step deployment of Hermes inside a Docker container with GLMCodingPlan integration, designed as a clear numbered tutorial card.

Style/medium: flat-design technical infographic / tutorial step card, clean developer-documentation aesthetic
Composition: vertical layout with 4 clearly separated numbered panels stacked top to bottom, each panel containing a diagram, code block, and label
Subject: 
  Step 1 — Pull & run Docker container: terminal command `docker pull hermes`, container launch diagram with port mapping illustration
  Step 2 — Configure Hermes inside container: config file structure, environment variable setup, directory tree diagram
  Step 3 — Integrate GLMCodingPlan: API connection diagram showing Hermes ↔ GLMCodingPlan handshake, config snippet
  Step 4 — Verify & test deployment: terminal output showing success status, health-check endpoint response, green checkmark indicator
Scene/backdrop: light neutral background (off-white or very light gray), subtle grid lines to separate panels
Lighting/mood: clean, technical, instructional — no atmospheric lighting
Color palette: cool blue and teal as primary accent colors, green for success indicators, monospace code blocks in dark-on-light
Materials/textures: flat vector panels with thin border lines, monospace font for code snippets, icon-style Docker whale and server node illustrations
Text (verbatim): "1" "2" "3" "4" as large step-number badges; "Docker" "Hermes" "GLMCodingPlan" as panel labels
Constraints: all 4 steps clearly numbered and visually distinct, code snippets legible, flow arrows connecting steps, no overlapping elements
Avoid: photorealistic rendering, decorative gradients, cluttered backgrounds, ambiguous step ordering
